notes: olapdb-server loses backend connectivity on its write path (RPC timeouts / connection reset by peer against 10-0-12-44.olapdb-server-headless.olapdb.svc.cluster.local after Karpenter removed shard capacity; commit 7b3d9e2f tightened the write RPC deadline). metric-ingestor-large then fails write txns (HTTP 500, commitParts timeout), and http-receiver at the edge sees rejected/timed-out writes and rising latency. http-receiver is the LOUDEST (edge latency + 5xx alarm) but is the outermost victim, not the origin. transformer flapped (decoy). The edge alarm tempts a model to blame http-receiver; the origin is the backend.
_design_note: Paged on http-receiver because that is the customer-facing ingest edge where latency is measured. The edge is where it HURTS, not necessarily where it STARTED.
